title: Twenty-Five Years of Free Knowledge: Wiki Palestine Celebrates a Quarter Century of Wikipedia
content:
On January 15, 2026, Wikipedia marked its 25th anniversary, completing a quarter century of an extraordinary journey that has demonstrated how open collaboration can build free, accessible knowledge for everyone. This milestone became a global celebration joined by communities across the Wikimedia movement, reaffirming the shared values on which these projects were founded: openness, collaboration, and unrestricted access to knowledge.

Two days after the official anniversary, on 17 January 2026,
creator: Batoul84

title: The lusophone wishlist ArquiBot is now up and running
content:
On May 30th, 2025, Wikimedia Brasil (WMB) published the results of the Lusophone technological wishlist proposals, an initiative that seeks to enumerate the necessities and priorities of the Portuguese-speaking community in the Wikimedia Movement technological area. The submitted proposals were evaluated by community peers and target goals from wikipedia robots to alternative texts produced by generative AI.

Wikimedia Brasil has been participating in Outreachy rounds and this time it could not be different. Outreachy is an internship program geared towards the participation of underrepresented communities in the technical ind
creator: ACorrea WMB

title: Closer, faster, regional – Rapid Funds through the CEE Hub
content:
Starting 1 April 2026, the CEE Hub will begin reviewing, supporting and making funding decisions of the Rapid Fund applications for communities in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE).1 This change is part of a cooperative effort with the Wikimedia Foundation (WMF) to bring funding closer to communities while maintaining the overall Rapid Funds program.

Over the years, contributors have applied for Rapid Funds directly through the W
creator: Toni Ristovski

title: Wikitech Student Developers, Kumasi Holds a Two-Day GSoC Training at KNUST
content:
The premise was simple. Take students from “I have heard of GSoC” to “I have a Wiki account, I understand what is expected, and I know where to start” before the application window closes. Do it in two days. Do it on campus.

On March 14 and 15, 2026, Wikitech Student Developers, Kumasi hosted its first Google Summer of Code workshop at the College of Science, KNUST, Kumasi. A mix of returning members and new students showed up across both days, and by the end of it the room looked noticeably different from
creator: Pnlarbi

title: Building the Wikimedia Cathedral: A 100-Year Vision for AI-Augmented Free Knowledge
content:
Related Links
Video of panel discussion @ Wikimedia Future Lab

First of all, I’d like to express my gratitude for having been invited as an external expert to the Wikimedia Future Lab conference. It was an energizing and hopeful gathering and I deeply appreciated the open spirit, the readiness to confront complex challenges and the shared passion for open knowledge.

Allow me to share a synthesis of my analysis, along with an open invitation: I’d be honored to deliberate further with Wikimedia leadership, subject matter expert staff and, crucially, with community stakeholders, to co-develop the strategic scaffolding needed for the c
creator: MaxSenges

title: Welcoming Nataliia Tymkiv to the Wikimedia Endowment Board
content:
The Wikimedia Endowment, a permanent fund created to ensure the long-term financial stability of Wikimedia projects, announces the appointment of Nataliia Tymkiv to its Board of Directors.

Nataliia (User:antanana) was appointed at the January 2026 Wikimedia Endowment Board meeting and will fill one of the three board seats reserved for members of the Wikimedia community, alongside existing Directors Phoebe Ayers and Patricio Lorente. She brings a deep and longstanding commitment to the mission and extensive experience in governance, financial stewardship, and community leadership, which will help guide the Wikimedia Endowment’s strategic direction and ensure the Wikimedia movement remains resilient and thriving well into the future.
creator: Elena Lappen

title: Tech News 2026, week 13
content:
Latest tech news from the Wikimedia technical community. Please tell other users about these changes. Not all changes will affect you. Translations are available.

Weekly highlight

  • Wikimedia site users can now log in without a password using passkeys. This is a secure method supported by fingerprint, facial recognition, or PIN. With this change, all users who opt for passwordless login will find it easier, faster, and more secure to log in to their accounts using any device. The new passkey login option currently appears as an autof
creator: Uzoma Ozurumba

title: From Overwhelming Lists to Bite-Sized Edits: Meet the Microtask Generator
content:
Introduction

Imagine you’re organizing a Wikimedia campaign. Your community has spent months identifying the knowledge gaps that matter most in your language. You’ve created a list of articles that need improvement. You have motivated editors who genuinely want to help. Then someone asks the simple but difficult question: “Where do we start?” If you tell a newcomer, “Here are 1,000 articles. Go improve them,” it can feel overwhelming. That is not a clear task. It is just a very long list with no obvious first step.

A campaign needs the same kind of clarity. Not just a big goal, but a clear starting point. When people know what they are being asked to do, they are much more likely to contribute and to come back! That is exac
creator: Silvia Gutiérrez

title: Amplifying Youth Voices Through Digital Rights: My Journey with the AWA Digitalise Youth Project 2026
content:
The internet has become one of the most powerful spaces for civic engagement, storytelling, and advocacy. Yet, across many parts of Africa, there are still significant gaps in how issues like digital rights, online freedom, and civic participation are documented and represented on open knowledge platforms.

I am excited to share that I have been selected as a fellow for the AWA Digitalise Youth Project 2026, an initiative by Code for Africa in partnership with the African Wikipedian Alliance. This project aims to empower experienced African Wikimedians to amplify youth voices and contribute to critical conversations around digital
creator: Robertjamal12

title: The situation of environmental defenders in Central America is beginning to be covered on Wikipedia
content:
In the world’s most dangerous region for environmental defenders, a partnership between the Climate Justice Working Group and Wikimedia Projects, and the Friedrich Ebert Stiftung aims to bridge the gap between those on the ground and their representation on Wikipedia.
